BIOS 6624: Project 3

Ryan Summers

2026-04-01

Load Data

fmhst_raw <- read_csv("/Users/ryan_summers/Library/Mobile Documents/com~apple~CloudDocs/GitHub/BIOS6624-Class/Project3/DataRaw/frmgham2.csv")
## Rows: 11627 Columns: 39
## ── Column specification ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
## Delimiter: ","
## dbl (39): RANDID, SEX, TOTCHOL, AGE, SYSBP, DIABP, CURSMOKE, CIGPDAY, BMI, D...
## 
## ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data.
## ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.

Data Management & Cleanup

Remove subjects who had event (stroke) at baseline and Filter to 10yrs cutoff. Calculate censored indicator if event doesn’t happen within 10yrs and calculate time to censoring variable.

cutoff_days <- 10*365.25

fmhst_filt <- fmhst_raw %>% 
  # remove subjects who had event prior to baseline assessment
  filter(PERIOD == 1, PREVSTRK == 0) %>%   # baseline cohort
  mutate(
    # time to event or being censored truncated at 10 years
    TIMESTRKorCENS_10y = pmin(TIMESTRK, cutoff_days),

    # event indicator within 10 years
    STROKE_10y = if_else(STROKE == 1 & TIMESTRK <= cutoff_days, 1, 0),
    
    ## all censored subjects
    ## **TIMESTRK includes censored participants if STROKE==0**
    CENSORED_10y = if_else(STROKE_10y == 0, 1, 0),
    
    # convert variables to factors
    SEX = factor(SEX, levels = c(1,2), labels = c("Men", "Women")),
    DIABETES = factor(DIABETES, levels = c(0,1), labels = c("No", "Yes")),
    PREVCHD = factor(PREVCHD, levels = c(0,1), labels = c("No", "Yes")),
    BPMEDS = factor(BPMEDS, levels = c(0,1), labels = c("No", "Yes")),
    CURSMOKE = factor(CURSMOKE, levels = c(0,1), labels = c("No", "Yes")),
    STROKE_10y = factor(STROKE_10y, levels = c(0,1), labels = c("No-Censored", "Yes")),
    STROKE_bin = ifelse(STROKE_10y == "Yes", 1, 0),
    CENSORED_10y = factor(CENSORED_10y, levels = c(0, 1), labels = c("No", "Yes")),
    TIMESTRKorCENS_10y_yrs = TIMESTRKorCENS_10y/365.25,
    ) %>% 
  mutate(
    SYSBP_10 = SYSBP / 10,
    TOTCHOL_10 = TOTCHOL / 10)

We restricted the primary analysis to baseline risk factors measured at period 1 and modeled incident stroke over the first 10 years of follow-up. This approach aligns predictor measurement more closely with the risk window of interest. We additionally examined how major risk factors changed across the three examination periods to assess whether a time-varying covariate analysis might be informative. If risk factors change materially over time, a baseline-only model may understate or misclassify the relationship between current risk-factor status and stroke hazard. However, time-varying covariate analyses in observational data may also be harder to interpret because updated covariates can be influenced by treatment changes and evolving disease status.

Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA)

Table1

tbl1 <- fmhst_filt %>%
  tbl_summary(
    by = SEX,
    include = c(
      AGE, SYSBP, DIABETES, TOTCHOL, PREVCHD, BPMEDS, CURSMOKE,
      BMI, STROKE_10y),
    type = list(
      all_continuous() ~ "continuous2",
      all_categorical() ~ "categorical"),
    statistic = list(
      all_continuous() ~ c(
        "{mean} ({sd})",
        "{median} ({p25}, {p75})",
        "{min} - {max}"),
      all_categorical() ~ "{n} ({p}%)"),
    digits = list(AGE ~ 0, SYSBP ~ 0),
    missing = "ifany",
    missing_text = "NA",
    missing_stat = "{N_miss} ({p_miss}%)",
    label = list(
      AGE ~ "Age",
      SYSBP ~ "Systolic BP",
      DIABETES ~ "Diabetes",
      TOTCHOL ~ "Total Cholesterol (mg/dL)",
      PREVCHD ~ "Prevalent Coronary Heart Disease",
      BPMEDS ~ "BP Meds",
      CURSMOKE ~ "Smoker",
      STROKE_10y ~ "Event (Stroke)")) %>%
  add_overall() %>% bold_labels() %>% italicize_levels() %>% add_n() %>%
  as_gt() %>%
  gt::fmt_markdown(columns = everything()) %>%
  tab_header(
    title = "Characteristics of the Framingham Heart Study Cohort") %>% 
  tab_options(
    table.width = pct(70),
    table.font.size = "small",
    heading.align = "left",
    data_row.padding = px(3),
    table.border.top.width = px(2),
    table.border.bottom.width = px(2))

  tbl_summary(
    by = SEX,
    include = c(
      AGE, SYSBP, DIABETES, TOTCHOL, PREVCHD, BPMEDS, CURSMOKE, BMI),
    type = list(
      all_continuous() ~ "continuous",
      all_categorical() ~ "categorical"),
    statistic = list(
      all_continuous() ~ "{mean} ({sd})",
      all_categorical() ~ "{n} ({p}%)"),

      AGE ~ 0,
      SYSBP ~ 0,
      TOTCHOL ~ 0,
      BMI ~ 1),
    missing = "ifany",

      AGE ~ "Age",
      SYSBP ~ "Systolic BP",
      DIABETES ~ "Diabetes",
      TOTCHOL ~ "Total Cholesterol (mg/dL)",
      PREVCHD ~ "Cardiovascular Disease",
      BPMEDS ~ "BP Meds",

  bold_labels() %>%

tbl1 <- fmhst_filt %>%
  tbl_summary(
    by = SEX,
    include = c(
      AGE, SYSBP, DIABETES, TOTCHOL, PREVCHD, BPMEDS, CURSMOKE, BMI
    ),
    type = list(
      all_continuous() ~ "continuous",
      all_categorical() ~ "categorical"
    ),
    statistic = list(
      all_continuous() ~ "{mean} ({sd})",
      all_categorical() ~ "{n} ({p}%)"
    ),
    digits = list(
      AGE ~ 0,
      SYSBP ~ 0,
      TOTCHOL ~ 0,
      BMI ~ 1
    ),
    missing = "no",
    label = list(
      AGE ~ "Age",
      SYSBP ~ "Systolic BP",
      DIABETES ~ "Diabetes",
      TOTCHOL ~ "Total Cholesterol (mg/dL)",
      PREVCHD ~ "Prevalent CHD",
      BPMEDS ~ "BP Meds",
      CURSMOKE ~ "Smoker",
      BMI ~ "BMI")) %>%
  add_overall() %>%
  add_n() %>%
  add_stat(
    fns = everything() ~ function(data, variable, ...) {
      x <- data[[variable]]
      paste0(sum(is.na(x)), " (", round(mean(is.na(x)) * 100, 1), "%)")
    },
    location = everything() ~ "label") %>%
  modify_header(add_stat_1 ~ "Missing") %>%
  modify_table_body(
    ~ dplyr::relocate(.x, add_stat_1, .after = n)) %>%
  bold_labels() %>%
  italicize_levels() %>%
  as_gt() %>% 
  tab_style(
    style = cell_text(weight = "bold"),
    locations = cells_column_labels(columns = add_stat_1)) %>% 
  gt::fmt_markdown(columns = everything()) %>%
  tab_header(
    title = "Characteristics of the Framingham Heart Study Cohort") %>%
  tab_options(
    table.width = pct(70),
    table.font.size = "small",
    heading.align = "left",
    data_row.padding = px(3))

# number of participants
cat("Number of participants in entire cohort", 
   length(unique(fmhst_raw$RANDID)), "\n\n")
## Number of participants in entire cohort 4434
cat("Number of participants in 10yr subset cohort", 
   length(unique(fmhst_filt$RANDID)), "\n\n")
## Number of participants in 10yr subset cohort 4402
hist(fmhst_filt$TOTCHOL)

# crude incidence
fmhst_filt %>%
  group_by(SEX) %>%
  summarise(
    events = sum(STROKE_10y == "Yes"),
    total = n(),
    risk = round( (events / total)*100,1 ))
## # A tibble: 2 × 4
##   SEX   events total  risk
##   <fct>  <int> <int> <dbl>
## 1 Men       49  1930   2.5
## 2 Women     62  2472   2.5

Correlations & Associations

corr_vars <- c("AGE", "SYSBP", "TOTCHOL", "BMI")

cor_matrix <- cor(
  fmhst_filt[, corr_vars],
  use = "pairwise.complete.obs",
  method = "spearman")

ggcorrplot(cor_matrix,
           type = "lower",
           lab = T)
## Warning: `aes_string()` was deprecated in ggplot2 3.0.0.
## ℹ Please use tidy evaluation idioms with `aes()`.
## ℹ See also `vignette("ggplot2-in-packages")` for more information.
## ℹ The deprecated feature was likely used in the ggcorrplot package.
##   Please report the issue at <https://github.com/kassambara/ggcorrplot/issues>.
## This warning is displayed once every 8 hours.
## Call `lifecycle::last_lifecycle_warnings()` to see where this warning was
## generated.

tab <- table(fmhst_filt$DIABETES, fmhst_filt$PREVCHD)
cramerV(tab)
## Cramer V 
##  0.04129

Test association of BP and BP medication use btw Women & Men

fmhst_complete <- fmhst_filt %>% filter(!is.na(SYSBP), !is.na(BPMEDS))
t.test(log(SYSBP) ~ BPMEDS, data = subset(fmhst_complete, SEX=="Women"))
## 
##  Welch Two Sample t-test
## 
## data:  log(SYSBP) by BPMEDS
## t = -13.593, df = 103.56, p-value < 2.2e-16
## alternative hypothesis: true difference in means between group No and group Yes is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  -0.2603942 -0.1940896
## sample estimates:
##  mean in group No mean in group Yes 
##          4.870545          5.097787
t.test(log(SYSBP) ~ BPMEDS, data = subset(fmhst_complete, SEX=="Men"))
## 
##  Welch Two Sample t-test
## 
## data:  log(SYSBP) by BPMEDS
## t = -8.5323, df = 40.276, p-value = 1.44e-10
## alternative hypothesis: true difference in means between group No and group Yes is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  -0.2642412 -0.1630493
## sample estimates:
##  mean in group No mean in group Yes 
##          4.864984          5.078629
ggplot(fmhst_complete, aes(x = BPMEDS, y = log(SYSBP), fill = BPMEDS)) +
  facet_wrap(~SEX) +
  geom_boxplot() + theme_bw()

Kaplan-Meier Curve

make_stroke_plot <- function(data, sex, vars, title = NULL) {
  plot_data <- data %>%
    filter(SEX == sex) %>%
    mutate(
      SBP_cat     = if_else(SYSBP >= 160, "BP≥160", "BP<160"),
      AGE_cat     = if_else(AGE >= 60, "Age≥60", "Age<60"),
      TOTCHOL_cat = if_else(TOTCHOL >= 240, "Chol≥240", "Chol<240"),
      BMI_cat     = if_else(BMI >= 30, "BMI≥30", "BMI<30")) %>%
    select(TIMESTRKorCENS_10y, STROKE_10y, all_of(names(vars))) %>%
    pivot_longer(
      cols = -c(TIMESTRKorCENS_10y, STROKE_10y),
      names_to = "variable",
      values_to = "group") %>%
    mutate(
      variable = factor(variable,
        levels = names(vars),
        labels = unname(vars)))

  fit <- survfit(
    Surv(TIMESTRKorCENS_10y, STROKE_10y == "Yes") ~ group + variable,
    data = plot_data)

  p <- ggsurvplot_facet(
    fit,
    data = plot_data,
    facet.by = "variable",
    conf.int = F,
    # pval = T,
    # pval.method = T,
    ylim = c(0.97, 1),
    xlab = "Time (Days)",
    ylab = "Stroke-free probability",
    title = title,
    ggtheme = theme_bw() )

  p <- p + theme(legend.position = "bottom")
  p
}
vars1 <- c(
  AGE_cat  = "Age",
  SBP_cat  = "Systolic BP",
  DIABETES = "Diabetes",
  PREVCHD  = "Prior CHD")

vars2 <- c(
  TOTCHOL_cat = "Total Chol",
  BMI_cat     = "BMI",
  BPMEDS      = "BP Meds",
  CURSMOKE    = "Smoker")


p_women1 <- make_stroke_plot(
  data = fmhst_filt,
  sex = "Women",
  vars = vars1,
  title = "Stroke-free survival: Women")

p_women2 <- make_stroke_plot(
  data = fmhst_filt,
  sex = "Women",
  vars = vars2,
  title = "Stroke-free survival: Women")

p_men1 <- make_stroke_plot(
  data = fmhst_filt,
  sex = "Men",
  vars = vars1,
  title = "Stroke-free survival: Men")

p_men2 <- make_stroke_plot(
  data = fmhst_filt,
  sex = "Men",
  vars = vars2,
  title = "Stroke-free survival: Men")

p_women1

p_women2

p_men1

Distributions of Time to Event by Sex

time_vars <- c(TIMESTRKorCENS_10y = "Follow-up time (days)")

# build summary table
time_tbl <- fmhst_filt %>%
  group_by(SEX) %>%
  summarise(
    `Time to Event` = "Follow-up time (days)",
    N = sum(!is.na(TIMESTRKorCENS_10y)),
    NMiss = sum(is.na(TIMESTRKorCENS_10y)),
    Mean = mean(TIMESTRKorCENS_10y, na.rm = TRUE),
    Std = sd(TIMESTRKorCENS_10y, na.rm = TRUE),
    Min = min(TIMESTRKorCENS_10y, na.rm = TRUE),
    P25 = quantile(TIMESTRKorCENS_10y, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
    Median = median(TIMESTRKorCENS_10y, na.rm = TRUE),
    P75 = quantile(TIMESTRKorCENS_10y, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
    Max = max(TIMESTRKorCENS_10y, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop") %>%
  # indent labels within sex groups
  mutate(SEX = if_else(duplicated(SEX), "", as.character(SEX))) %>%
  gt(groupname_col = "SEX") %>%
  tab_header(title = "Distributions of Time to Event by Sex") %>%
  cols_label(
    `Time to Event` = "Time to Event",
    N = "N",
    NMiss = "NMiss",
    Mean = "Mean",
    Std = "Std",
    Min = "Min",
    P25 = "P25",
    Median = "Median",
    P75 = "P75",
    Max = "Max") %>%
  fmt_number(
    columns = c(Mean, Std, Min, P25, Median, P75, Max),
    decimals = 0) %>%
  cols_align(
    align = "left",
    columns = c(`Time to Event`)) %>%
  cols_align(
    align = "center",
    columns = c(N, NMiss, Mean, Std, Min, P25, Median, P75, Max)) %>%
  tab_options(
    table.font.size = px(16),
    heading.align = "left",
    data_row.padding = px(6),
    row_group.font.weight = "normal",
    column_labels.font.weight = "normal",
    table.border.top.width = px(1),
    table.border.bottom.width = px(1))

time_tbl
Distributions of Time to Event by Sex
Time to Event N NMiss Mean Std Min P25 Median P75 Max
Men
Follow-up time (days) 1930 0 3,432 665 26 3,652 3,652 3,652 3,652
Women
Follow-up time (days) 2472 0 3,505 563 22 3,652 3,652 3,652 3,652

Primary Analysis

Risk Factors

Variable Selection

Proportional assumption - low p-values indicates variable has time dependency “the proportionality assumption does not check linearity - the Cox PH model is semi-parametric and thus makes no assumption as to the form of the hazard. The proportionality assumption is that the hazard rate of an individual is relatively constant in time” * semi-parametric = don’t have to choose prob dist’n for surv time

# stratify datae in women and men
vars <- c("AGE", "SYSBP_10", "DIABETES", "TOTCHOL_10",
          "BMI", "CURSMOKE", "BPMEDS", "PREVCHD",
          "TIMESTRKorCENS_10y", "STROKE_bin")

men_dat <- subset(fmhst_filt, SEX == "Men", select = vars)
men_dat <- na.omit(men_dat)

women_dat <- subset(fmhst_filt, SEX == "Women", select = vars)
women_dat <- na.omit(women_dat)

Full model (Men)

cox.men.model <- coxph(
  Surv(TIMESTRKorCENS_10y, STROKE_bin) ~ AGE + SYSBP_10*BPMEDS + DIABETES + 
    # variables to be determined
    TOTCHOL_10 + BMI + CURSMOKE + PREVCHD, 
  data = men_dat)

summary(cox.men.model)
## Call:
## coxph(formula = Surv(TIMESTRKorCENS_10y, STROKE_bin) ~ AGE + 
##     SYSBP_10 * BPMEDS + DIABETES + TOTCHOL_10 + BMI + CURSMOKE + 
##     PREVCHD, data = men_dat)
## 
##   n= 1897, number of events= 48 
## 
##                          coef  exp(coef)   se(coef)      z Pr(>|z|)    
## AGE                 6.503e-02  1.067e+00  1.983e-02  3.279 0.001040 ** 
## SYSBP_10            3.475e-01  1.416e+00  6.127e-02  5.672 1.41e-08 ***
## BPMEDSYes           8.096e+00  3.281e+03  3.304e+00  2.450 0.014284 *  
## DIABETESYes         1.626e+00  5.082e+00  4.291e-01  3.788 0.000152 ***
## TOTCHOL_10          1.960e-02  1.020e+00  3.139e-02  0.624 0.532337    
## BMI                -4.282e-03  9.957e-01  4.410e-02 -0.097 0.922650    
## CURSMOKEYes         6.616e-01  1.938e+00  3.233e-01  2.046 0.040710 *  
## PREVCHDYes          3.925e-03  1.004e+00  5.417e-01  0.007 0.994218    
## SYSBP_10:BPMEDSYes -4.393e-01  6.445e-01  2.073e-01 -2.120 0.034046 *  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
##                    exp(coef) exp(-coef) lower .95 upper .95
## AGE                   1.0672  0.9370402    1.0265 1.109e+00
## SYSBP_10              1.4156  0.7064246    1.2554 1.596e+00
## BPMEDSYes          3280.7394  0.0003048    5.0501 2.131e+06
## DIABETESYes           5.0816  0.1967880    2.1914 1.178e+01
## TOTCHOL_10            1.0198  0.9805898    0.9589 1.085e+00
## BMI                   0.9957  1.0042913    0.9133 1.086e+00
## CURSMOKEYes           1.9379  0.5160283    1.0284 3.652e+00
## PREVCHDYes            1.0039  0.9960825    0.3472 2.902e+00
## SYSBP_10:BPMEDSYes    0.6445  1.5516639    0.4293 9.675e-01
## 
## Concordance= 0.808  (se = 0.031 )
## Likelihood ratio test= 75.09  on 9 df,   p=2e-12
## Wald test            = 82.25  on 9 df,   p=6e-14
## Score (logrank) test = 105.9  on 9 df,   p=<2e-16

StepReg:

form <- Surv(TIMESTRKorCENS_10y, STROKE_bin) ~ AGE + SYSBP_10*BPMEDS + DIABETES + 
    TOTCHOL_10 + BMI + CURSMOKE + PREVCHD

step.fit.men <- stepwise(formula = form,
                data = men_dat,
                type = "cox",
                include = c("AGE", "DIABETES", "SYSBP_10"),
                strategy = "backward",
                metric = c("AICc", "SBC", "HQ"))

summary(step.fit.men$backward$AICc)
## Call:
## coxph(formula = Surv(TIMESTRKorCENS_10y, STROKE_bin) ~ AGE + 
##     DIABETES + SYSBP_10 + CURSMOKE, data = data, weights = NULL, 
##     method = "efron")
## 
##   n= 1897, number of events= 48 
## 
##                coef exp(coef) se(coef)     z Pr(>|z|)    
## AGE         0.06420   1.06630  0.01906 3.368 0.000757 ***
## DIABETESYes 1.56998   4.80654  0.42263 3.715 0.000203 ***
## SYSBP_10    0.33458   1.39735  0.05736 5.833 5.45e-09 ***
## CURSMOKEYes 0.65458   1.92433  0.31570 2.073 0.038133 *  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
##             exp(coef) exp(-coef) lower .95 upper .95
## AGE             1.066     0.9378     1.027     1.107
## DIABETESYes     4.807     0.2080     2.099    11.005
## SYSBP_10        1.397     0.7156     1.249     1.564
## CURSMOKEYes     1.924     0.5197     1.036     3.573
## 
## Concordance= 0.797  (se = 0.031 )
## Likelihood ratio test= 69.32  on 4 df,   p=3e-14
## Wald test            = 78.97  on 4 df,   p=3e-16
## Score (logrank) test = 97.01  on 4 df,   p=<2e-16
summary(step.fit.men$backward$SBC)
## Call:
## coxph(formula = Surv(TIMESTRKorCENS_10y, STROKE_bin) ~ AGE + 
##     DIABETES + SYSBP_10 + CURSMOKE, data = data, weights = NULL, 
##     method = "efron")
## 
##   n= 1897, number of events= 48 
## 
##                coef exp(coef) se(coef)     z Pr(>|z|)    
## AGE         0.06420   1.06630  0.01906 3.368 0.000757 ***
## DIABETESYes 1.56998   4.80654  0.42263 3.715 0.000203 ***
## SYSBP_10    0.33458   1.39735  0.05736 5.833 5.45e-09 ***
## CURSMOKEYes 0.65458   1.92433  0.31570 2.073 0.038133 *  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
##             exp(coef) exp(-coef) lower .95 upper .95
## AGE             1.066     0.9378     1.027     1.107
## DIABETESYes     4.807     0.2080     2.099    11.005
## SYSBP_10        1.397     0.7156     1.249     1.564
## CURSMOKEYes     1.924     0.5197     1.036     3.573
## 
## Concordance= 0.797  (se = 0.031 )
## Likelihood ratio test= 69.32  on 4 df,   p=3e-14
## Wald test            = 78.97  on 4 df,   p=3e-16
## Score (logrank) test = 97.01  on 4 df,   p=<2e-16
summary(step.fit.men$backward$HQ)
## Call:
## coxph(formula = Surv(TIMESTRKorCENS_10y, STROKE_bin) ~ AGE + 
##     DIABETES + SYSBP_10 + CURSMOKE, data = data, weights = NULL, 
##     method = "efron")
## 
##   n= 1897, number of events= 48 
## 
##                coef exp(coef) se(coef)     z Pr(>|z|)    
## AGE         0.06420   1.06630  0.01906 3.368 0.000757 ***
## DIABETESYes 1.56998   4.80654  0.42263 3.715 0.000203 ***
## SYSBP_10    0.33458   1.39735  0.05736 5.833 5.45e-09 ***
## CURSMOKEYes 0.65458   1.92433  0.31570 2.073 0.038133 *  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
##             exp(coef) exp(-coef) lower .95 upper .95
## AGE             1.066     0.9378     1.027     1.107
## DIABETESYes     4.807     0.2080     2.099    11.005
## SYSBP_10        1.397     0.7156     1.249     1.564
## CURSMOKEYes     1.924     0.5197     1.036     3.573
## 
## Concordance= 0.797  (se = 0.031 )
## Likelihood ratio test= 69.32  on 4 df,   p=3e-14
## Wald test            = 78.97  on 4 df,   p=3e-16
## Score (logrank) test = 97.01  on 4 df,   p=<2e-16
performance(step.fit.men)
##                                                                         model
## 1 Surv(TIMESTRKorCENS_10y, STROKE_bin) ~ AGE + DIABETES + SYSBP_10 + CURSMOKE
##                            strategy:metric c-index_train c-index_test auc_sh
## 1 backward:AICc; backward:SBC; backward:HQ        0.7971           NA     NA
summary(step.fit.men$backward$AICc)
## Call:
## coxph(formula = Surv(TIMESTRKorCENS_10y, STROKE_bin) ~ AGE + 
##     DIABETES + SYSBP_10 + CURSMOKE, data = data, weights = NULL, 
##     method = "efron")
## 
##   n= 1897, number of events= 48 
## 
##                coef exp(coef) se(coef)     z Pr(>|z|)    
## AGE         0.06420   1.06630  0.01906 3.368 0.000757 ***
## DIABETESYes 1.56998   4.80654  0.42263 3.715 0.000203 ***
## SYSBP_10    0.33458   1.39735  0.05736 5.833 5.45e-09 ***
## CURSMOKEYes 0.65458   1.92433  0.31570 2.073 0.038133 *  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
##             exp(coef) exp(-coef) lower .95 upper .95
## AGE             1.066     0.9378     1.027     1.107
## DIABETESYes     4.807     0.2080     2.099    11.005
## SYSBP_10        1.397     0.7156     1.249     1.564
## CURSMOKEYes     1.924     0.5197     1.036     3.573
## 
## Concordance= 0.797  (se = 0.031 )
## Likelihood ratio test= 69.32  on 4 df,   p=3e-14
## Wald test            = 78.97  on 4 df,   p=3e-16
## Score (logrank) test = 97.01  on 4 df,   p=<2e-16
summary(step.fit.men$backward$SL)
## Length  Class   Mode 
##      0   NULL   NULL
plot(step.fit.men, strategy = "backward", process = "overview")

plot(step.fit.men, strategy = "backward", process = "detail")

Plot details of stepwise

plot_list <- setNames(
  lapply(c("backward"),function(i){
    setNames(
      lapply(c("detail","overview"),function(j){
        plot(step.fit.men, strategy=i, process=j)
    }),
    c("detail","overview"))
  }),
  c("backward"))

cowplot::plot_grid(plotlist = plot_list$backward, ncol = 1, 
                   rel_heights = c(2, 1))

step.men <- step(cox.men.model, 
     direction = "backward", 
     scope = list(lower = ~ AGE + SYSBP_10 + DIABETES))
## Start:  AIC=660.2
## Surv(TIMESTRKorCENS_10y, STROKE_bin) ~ AGE + SYSBP_10 * BPMEDS + 
##     DIABETES + TOTCHOL_10 + BMI + CURSMOKE + PREVCHD
## 
##                   Df    AIC
## - PREVCHD          1 658.20
## - BMI              1 658.21
## - TOTCHOL_10       1 658.57
## <none>               660.20
## - SYSBP_10:BPMEDS  1 662.26
## - CURSMOKE         1 662.58
## 
## Step:  AIC=658.2
## Surv(TIMESTRKorCENS_10y, STROKE_bin) ~ AGE + SYSBP_10 + BPMEDS + 
##     DIABETES + TOTCHOL_10 + BMI + CURSMOKE + SYSBP_10:BPMEDS
## 
##                   Df    AIC
## - BMI              1 656.21
## - TOTCHOL_10       1 656.57
## <none>               658.20
## - SYSBP_10:BPMEDS  1 660.27
## - CURSMOKE         1 660.59
## 
## Step:  AIC=656.21
## Surv(TIMESTRKorCENS_10y, STROKE_bin) ~ AGE + SYSBP_10 + BPMEDS + 
##     DIABETES + TOTCHOL_10 + CURSMOKE + SYSBP_10:BPMEDS
## 
##                   Df    AIC
## - TOTCHOL_10       1 654.58
## <none>               656.21
## - SYSBP_10:BPMEDS  1 658.29
## - CURSMOKE         1 658.81
## 
## Step:  AIC=654.58
## Surv(TIMESTRKorCENS_10y, STROKE_bin) ~ AGE + SYSBP_10 + BPMEDS + 
##     DIABETES + CURSMOKE + SYSBP_10:BPMEDS
## 
##                   Df    AIC
## <none>               654.58
## - SYSBP_10:BPMEDS  1 656.54
## - CURSMOKE         1 657.31
summary(step.men)
## Call:
## coxph(formula = Surv(TIMESTRKorCENS_10y, STROKE_bin) ~ AGE + 
##     SYSBP_10 + BPMEDS + DIABETES + CURSMOKE + SYSBP_10:BPMEDS, 
##     data = men_dat)
## 
##   n= 1897, number of events= 48 
## 
##                          coef  exp(coef)   se(coef)      z Pr(>|z|)    
## AGE                   0.06401    1.06610    0.01903  3.364 0.000769 ***
## SYSBP_10              0.34616    1.41363    0.06035  5.736 9.69e-09 ***
## BPMEDSYes             7.95285 2843.66499    3.26015  2.439 0.014711 *  
## DIABETESYes           1.61708    5.03836    0.42606  3.795 0.000147 ***
## CURSMOKEYes           0.67535    1.96473    0.31785  2.125 0.033606 *  
## SYSBP_10:BPMEDSYes   -0.43045    0.65022    0.20462 -2.104 0.035414 *  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
##                    exp(coef) exp(-coef) lower .95 upper .95
## AGE                   1.0661  0.9379998    1.0271 1.107e+00
## SYSBP_10              1.4136  0.7073999    1.2559 1.591e+00
## BPMEDSYes          2843.6650  0.0003517    4.7735 1.694e+06
## DIABETESYes           5.0384  0.1984773    2.1859 1.161e+01
## CURSMOKEYes           1.9647  0.5089769    1.0538 3.663e+00
## SYSBP_10:BPMEDSYes    0.6502  1.5379439    0.4354 9.710e-01
## 
## Concordance= 0.806  (se = 0.031 )
## Likelihood ratio test= 74.72  on 6 df,   p=4e-14
## Wald test            = 82.98  on 6 df,   p=9e-16
## Score (logrank) test = 105.4  on 6 df,   p=<2e-16

Full model (Women)

cox.women.model <- coxph(
  Surv(TIMESTRKorCENS_10y, STROKE_bin) ~ AGE + SYSBP_10*BPMEDS + DIABETES + 
    # variables to be determined
    TOTCHOL_10 + BMI + CURSMOKE + PREVCHD, 
  data = women_dat)

summary(cox.women.model)
## Call:
## coxph(formula = Surv(TIMESTRKorCENS_10y, STROKE_bin) ~ AGE + 
##     SYSBP_10 * BPMEDS + DIABETES + TOTCHOL_10 + BMI + CURSMOKE + 
##     PREVCHD, data = women_dat)
## 
##   n= 2378, number of events= 57 
## 
##                        coef exp(coef) se(coef)      z Pr(>|z|)    
## AGE                 0.07429   1.07712  0.02051  3.622 0.000293 ***
## SYSBP_10            0.25376   1.28886  0.05079  4.996 5.84e-07 ***
## BPMEDSYes           0.71308   2.04027  2.18841  0.326 0.744542    
## DIABETESYes         0.61903   1.85713  0.52438  1.181 0.237799    
## TOTCHOL_10         -0.01584   0.98429  0.03020 -0.524 0.599975    
## BMI                -0.01026   0.98979  0.02668 -0.384 0.700617    
## CURSMOKEYes         0.35588   1.42743  0.28870  1.233 0.217688    
## PREVCHDYes          0.47606   1.60971  0.44642  1.066 0.286253    
## SYSBP_10:BPMEDSYes -0.01574   0.98438  0.11838 -0.133 0.894212    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
##                    exp(coef) exp(-coef) lower .95 upper .95
## AGE                   1.0771     0.9284   1.03467     1.121
## SYSBP_10              1.2889     0.7759   1.16674     1.424
## BPMEDSYes             2.0403     0.4901   0.02798   148.752
## DIABETESYes           1.8571     0.5385   0.66449     5.190
## TOTCHOL_10            0.9843     1.0160   0.92773     1.044
## BMI                   0.9898     1.0103   0.93936     1.043
## CURSMOKEYes           1.4274     0.7006   0.81062     2.514
## PREVCHDYes            1.6097     0.6212   0.67105     3.861
## SYSBP_10:BPMEDSYes    0.9844     1.0159   0.78055     1.241
## 
## Concordance= 0.802  (se = 0.03 )
## Likelihood ratio test= 83.2  on 9 df,   p=4e-14
## Wald test            = 89.66  on 9 df,   p=2e-15
## Score (logrank) test = 128.4  on 9 df,   p=<2e-16
step.fit.women <- stepwise(formula = form,
                data = women_dat,
                type = "cox",
                include = c("AGE", "DIABETES", "SYSBP_10"),
                strategy = "backward",
                metric = c("AICc", "SBC", "HQ"))

summary(step.fit.women$backward$AICc)
## Call:
## coxph(formula = Surv(TIMESTRKorCENS_10y, STROKE_bin) ~ AGE + 
##     DIABETES + SYSBP_10 + CURSMOKE, data = data, weights = NULL, 
##     method = "efron")
## 
##   n= 2378, number of events= 57 
## 
##                coef exp(coef) se(coef)     z Pr(>|z|)    
## AGE         0.07545   1.07837  0.01996 3.781 0.000156 ***
## DIABETESYes 0.59400   1.81122  0.52125 1.140 0.254465    
## SYSBP_10    0.26039   1.29743  0.04141 6.288 3.21e-10 ***
## CURSMOKEYes 0.36149   1.43546  0.28623 1.263 0.206614    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
##             exp(coef) exp(-coef) lower .95 upper .95
## AGE             1.078     0.9273    1.0370     1.121
## DIABETESYes     1.811     0.5521    0.6521     5.031
## SYSBP_10        1.297     0.7708    1.1963     1.407
## CURSMOKEYes     1.435     0.6966    0.8191     2.516
## 
## Concordance= 0.799  (se = 0.029 )
## Likelihood ratio test= 80.39  on 4 df,   p=<2e-16
## Wald test            = 85.73  on 4 df,   p=<2e-16
## Score (logrank) test = 100.5  on 4 df,   p=<2e-16
summary(step.fit.women$backward$SBC)
## Call:
## coxph(formula = Surv(TIMESTRKorCENS_10y, STROKE_bin) ~ AGE + 
##     DIABETES + SYSBP_10 + CURSMOKE, data = data, weights = NULL, 
##     method = "efron")
## 
##   n= 2378, number of events= 57 
## 
##                coef exp(coef) se(coef)     z Pr(>|z|)    
## AGE         0.07545   1.07837  0.01996 3.781 0.000156 ***
## DIABETESYes 0.59400   1.81122  0.52125 1.140 0.254465    
## SYSBP_10    0.26039   1.29743  0.04141 6.288 3.21e-10 ***
## CURSMOKEYes 0.36149   1.43546  0.28623 1.263 0.206614    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
##             exp(coef) exp(-coef) lower .95 upper .95
## AGE             1.078     0.9273    1.0370     1.121
## DIABETESYes     1.811     0.5521    0.6521     5.031
## SYSBP_10        1.297     0.7708    1.1963     1.407
## CURSMOKEYes     1.435     0.6966    0.8191     2.516
## 
## Concordance= 0.799  (se = 0.029 )
## Likelihood ratio test= 80.39  on 4 df,   p=<2e-16
## Wald test            = 85.73  on 4 df,   p=<2e-16
## Score (logrank) test = 100.5  on 4 df,   p=<2e-16
summary(step.fit.women$backward$HQ)
## Call:
## coxph(formula = Surv(TIMESTRKorCENS_10y, STROKE_bin) ~ AGE + 
##     DIABETES + SYSBP_10 + CURSMOKE, data = data, weights = NULL, 
##     method = "efron")
## 
##   n= 2378, number of events= 57 
## 
##                coef exp(coef) se(coef)     z Pr(>|z|)    
## AGE         0.07545   1.07837  0.01996 3.781 0.000156 ***
## DIABETESYes 0.59400   1.81122  0.52125 1.140 0.254465    
## SYSBP_10    0.26039   1.29743  0.04141 6.288 3.21e-10 ***
## CURSMOKEYes 0.36149   1.43546  0.28623 1.263 0.206614    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
##             exp(coef) exp(-coef) lower .95 upper .95
## AGE             1.078     0.9273    1.0370     1.121
## DIABETESYes     1.811     0.5521    0.6521     5.031
## SYSBP_10        1.297     0.7708    1.1963     1.407
## CURSMOKEYes     1.435     0.6966    0.8191     2.516
## 
## Concordance= 0.799  (se = 0.029 )
## Likelihood ratio test= 80.39  on 4 df,   p=<2e-16
## Wald test            = 85.73  on 4 df,   p=<2e-16
## Score (logrank) test = 100.5  on 4 df,   p=<2e-16
performance(step.fit.women)
##                                                                         model
## 1 Surv(TIMESTRKorCENS_10y, STROKE_bin) ~ AGE + DIABETES + SYSBP_10 + CURSMOKE
##                            strategy:metric c-index_train c-index_test auc_sh
## 1 backward:AICc; backward:SBC; backward:HQ        0.7991           NA     NA
plot_list2 <- setNames(
  lapply(c("backward"), function(i){
    setNames(
      lapply(c("detail","overview"), function(j){
        plot(step.fit.women, strategy=i, process=j)
    }),
    c("detail","overview"))
  }),
  c("backward"))

cowplot::plot_grid(plotlist = plot_list2$backward, ncol = 2, 
                   rel_heights = c(2, 1))

step.women <- step(cox.women.model, 
     direction = "backward", 
     scope = list(lower = ~ AGE + SYSBP_10 + DIABETES))
## Start:  AIC=815.93
## Surv(TIMESTRKorCENS_10y, STROKE_bin) ~ AGE + SYSBP_10 * BPMEDS + 
##     DIABETES + TOTCHOL_10 + BMI + CURSMOKE + PREVCHD

## - SYSBP_10:BPMEDS  1 813.95

## - TOTCHOL_10  1 810.35
## - PREVCHD     1 811.13

##

##            Df    AIC

## - CURSMOKE  1 809.07
## <none>        809.43
## 
## Step:  AIC=808.74
## Surv(TIMESTRKorCENS_10y, STROKE_bin) ~ AGE + SYSBP_10 + DIABETES + 
##     CURSMOKE
## 
##            Df    AIC
## - CURSMOKE  1 808.28
## <none>        808.74
## 
## Step:  AIC=808.28
## Surv(TIMESTRKorCENS_10y, STROKE_bin) ~ AGE + SYSBP_10 + DIABETES
summary(step.women)
## Call:
## coxph(formula = Surv(TIMESTRKorCENS_10y, STROKE_bin) ~ AGE + 
##     SYSBP_10 + DIABETES, data = women_dat)
## 
##   n= 2378, number of events= 57 
## 
##                coef exp(coef) se(coef)     z Pr(>|z|)    
## AGE         0.07092   1.07350  0.01961 3.617 0.000298 ***
## SYSBP_10    0.25706   1.29313  0.04110 6.254    4e-10 ***
## DIABETESYes 0.56911   1.76669  0.52093 1.092 0.274623    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
##             exp(coef) exp(-coef) lower .95 upper .95
## AGE             1.073     0.9315    1.0330     1.116
## SYSBP_10        1.293     0.7733    1.1930     1.402
## DIABETESYes     1.767     0.5660    0.6364     4.904
## 
## Concordance= 0.796  (se = 0.03 )
## Likelihood ratio test= 78.85  on 3 df,   p=<2e-16
## Wald test            = 84.64  on 3 df,   p=<2e-16
## Score (logrank) test = 98.71  on 3 df,   p=<2e-16
#amount of women on BP meds
fmhst_filt %>% 
  filter(SEX=="Women") %>% 
  group_by(BPMEDS) %>% 
  summarize(n = n())
## # A tibble: 3 × 2
##   BPMEDS     n
##   <fct>  <int>
## 1 No      2338
## 2 Yes       96
## 3 <NA>      38

Final Models

cox.women.final <- coxph(
  Surv(TIMESTRKorCENS_10y, STROKE_bin) ~ AGE + SYSBP_10*BPMEDS + DIABETES + 
    CURSMOKE + PREVCHD, 
  data = women_dat)

summary(cox.women.final)
## Call:
## coxph(formula = Surv(TIMESTRKorCENS_10y, STROKE_bin) ~ AGE + 
##     SYSBP_10 * BPMEDS + DIABETES + CURSMOKE + PREVCHD, data = women_dat)
## 
##   n= 2378, number of events= 57 
## 
##                        coef exp(coef) se(coef)      z Pr(>|z|)    
## AGE                 0.07243   1.07511  0.02028  3.571 0.000356 ***
## SYSBP_10            0.24666   1.27974  0.04802  5.137 2.79e-07 ***
## BPMEDSYes           0.59697   1.81661  2.16522  0.276 0.782770    
## DIABETESYes         0.59546   1.81387  0.52198  1.141 0.253967    
## CURSMOKEYes         0.37311   1.45224  0.28681  1.301 0.193288    
## PREVCHDYes          0.50273   1.65323  0.44337  1.134 0.256843    
## SYSBP_10:BPMEDSYes -0.01002   0.99003  0.11692 -0.086 0.931730    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
##                    exp(coef) exp(-coef) lower .95 upper .95
## AGE                    1.075     0.9301   1.03321     1.119
## SYSBP_10               1.280     0.7814   1.16480     1.406
## BPMEDSYes              1.817     0.5505   0.02608   126.561
## DIABETESYes            1.814     0.5513   0.65207     5.046
## CURSMOKEYes            1.452     0.6886   0.82777     2.548
## PREVCHDYes             1.653     0.6049   0.69332     3.942
## SYSBP_10:BPMEDSYes     0.990     1.0101   0.78728     1.245
## 
## Concordance= 0.803  (se = 0.029 )
## Likelihood ratio test= 82.78  on 7 df,   p=4e-15
## Wald test            = 88.13  on 7 df,   p=3e-16
## Score (logrank) test = 126  on 7 df,   p=<2e-16
cox.men.final <- coxph(
  Surv(TIMESTRKorCENS_10y, STROKE_bin) ~ AGE + SYSBP_10*BPMEDS + DIABETES + 
    CURSMOKE + PREVCHD, 
  data = men_dat)

summary(cox.men.final)
## Call:
## coxph(formula = Surv(TIMESTRKorCENS_10y, STROKE_bin) ~ AGE + 
##     SYSBP_10 * BPMEDS + DIABETES + CURSMOKE + PREVCHD, data = men_dat)
## 
##   n= 1897, number of events= 48 
## 
##                          coef  exp(coef)   se(coef)      z Pr(>|z|)    
## AGE                   0.06383    1.06591    0.01942  3.287 0.001013 ** 
## SYSBP_10              0.34635    1.41389    0.06051  5.724 1.04e-08 ***
## BPMEDSYes             7.94601 2824.27058    3.26353  2.435 0.014900 *  
## DIABETESYes           1.61627    5.03426    0.42654  3.789 0.000151 ***
## CURSMOKEYes           0.67571    1.96544    0.31802  2.125 0.033605 *  
## PREVCHDYes            0.02406    1.02435    0.54012  0.045 0.964473    
## SYSBP_10:BPMEDSYes   -0.43018    0.65039    0.20470 -2.102 0.035596 *  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
##                    exp(coef) exp(-coef) lower .95 upper .95
## AGE                   1.0659  0.9381621    1.0261 1.107e+00
## SYSBP_10              1.4139  0.7072666    1.2558 1.592e+00
## BPMEDSYes          2824.2706  0.0003541    4.7096 1.694e+06
## DIABETESYes           5.0343  0.1986391    2.1820 1.161e+01
## CURSMOKEYes           1.9654  0.5087926    1.0538 3.666e+00
## PREVCHDYes            1.0243  0.9762300    0.3554 2.953e+00
## SYSBP_10:BPMEDSYes    0.6504  1.5375292    0.4354 9.714e-01
## 
## Concordance= 0.806  (se = 0.031 )
## Likelihood ratio test= 74.72  on 7 df,   p=2e-13
## Wald test            = 83.01  on 7 df,   p=3e-15
## Score (logrank) test = 105.4  on 7 df,   p=<2e-16

Sample interpretation: Systolic BP was positively associated with stroke risk in women, with each 1 mmHg increase corresponding to a 2.5% increase in hazard (HR = 1.025; 95% CI: 1.015–1.035). This corresponds to approximately a 28% (\(1.025^{10} ≈ 1.28\)) increase in hazard per 10 mmHg increase.

Systolic BP was positively associated with stroke risk in men, with each 1 mmHg increase corresponding to a 3.5% increase in hazard (HR = 1.0352; 95% CI: 1.023–1.048). This corresponds to approximately a 41.3% (\(1.035^{10} ≈ 1.413\)) increase in hazard per 10 mmHg increase.

Proportionality Check

ph_women <- cox.zph(cox.women.final)
ph_women
##                   chisq df     p
## AGE             0.04388  1 0.834
## SYSBP_10        0.04868  1 0.825
## BPMEDS          3.11644  1 0.078
## DIABETES        0.09397  1 0.759
## CURSMOKE        0.14328  1 0.705
## PREVCHD         0.00375  1 0.951
## SYSBP_10:BPMEDS 2.61833  1 0.106
## GLOBAL          4.13512  7 0.764
par(mfrow = c(3, 3), mar = c(4, 4, 2, 2)) # c(bottom, left, top, right)
plot(ph_women)

ph_men <- cox.zph(cox.men.final)
ph_men
##                  chisq df     p
## AGE             0.4734  1 0.491
## SYSBP_10        1.4618  1 0.227
## BPMEDS          0.0620  1 0.803
## DIABETES        2.7630  1 0.096
## CURSMOKE        0.0375  1 0.846
## PREVCHD         0.2726  1 0.602
## SYSBP_10:BPMEDS 0.1084  1 0.742
## GLOBAL          4.8993  7 0.672
par(mfrow = c(3, 3), mar = c(4, 4, 2, 2)) # c(bottom, left, top, right)
plot(ph_men)

Risk Factors Table

broom::tidy(cox.men.final, exponentiate = TRUE, conf.int = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(
    pct_change = (estimate - 1) * 100,
    pct_label = paste0(
      ifelse(pct_change >= 0, "+", ""),
      round(pct_change, 1), "%"))
## # A tibble: 7 × 9
##   term        estimate std.error statistic p.value conf.low conf.high pct_change
##   <chr>          <dbl>     <dbl>     <dbl>   <dbl>    <dbl>     <dbl>      <dbl>
## 1 AGE            1.07     0.0194    3.29   1.01e-3    1.03    1.11e+0       6.59
## 2 SYSBP_10       1.41     0.0605    5.72   1.04e-8    1.26    1.59e+0      41.4 
## 3 BPMEDSYes   2824.       3.26      2.43   1.49e-2    4.71    1.69e+6  282327.  
## 4 DIABETESYes    5.03     0.427     3.79   1.51e-4    2.18    1.16e+1     403.  
## 5 CURSMOKEYes    1.97     0.318     2.12   3.36e-2    1.05    3.67e+0      96.5 
## 6 PREVCHDYes     1.02     0.540     0.0445 9.64e-1    0.355   2.95e+0       2.43
## 7 SYSBP_10:B…    0.650    0.205    -2.10   3.56e-2    0.435   9.71e-1     -35.0 
## # ℹ 1 more variable: pct_label <chr>
# helper function to generate results table
make_cox_table <- function(model, sex_label) {
  tidy(model, exponentiate = TRUE, conf.int = TRUE) %>%
    filter(term != "(Intercept)") %>%
    mutate(
      # rename terms
      term = recode(
        term,
        "AGE" = "Age (per 1 year)",
        "SYSBP_10" = "Systolic BP (per 10 mmHg)",
        "DIABETESYes" = "Diabetes (Yes vs No)",
        "TOTCHOL" = "Total cholesterol (per 1 mg/dL)",
        "BMI" = "BMI (per 1 kg/m²)",
        "CURSMOKEYes" = "Current smoker (Yes vs No)",
        "BPMEDSYes" = "BP meds (Yes vs No)",
        "PREVCHDYes" = "Cardiovascular Disease (Yes vs No)",
        "SYSBP_10:BPMEDSYes" = "SBP × BP meds interaction"),

      # percent change in hazard
      pct_change = (estimate - 1) * 100,

      # formatted columns
      hr_ci = sprintf("%.2f (%.2f, %.2f; p=%s)",estimate,conf.low,conf.high,
                      format.pval(p.value, digits = 2, eps = 0.01)),
      pct_label = sprintf(
        "%s%.1f%%",
        ifelse(pct_change >= 0, "+", ""),
        pct_change)) %>%
    select(term, hr_ci, pct_label) %>%
    rename(
      !!paste0(sex_label, "_HR_CI") := hr_ci,
      !!paste0(sex_label, "_Pct") := pct_label)
  }

tbl_women <- make_cox_table(cox.women.final, "Women")
tbl_men   <- make_cox_table(cox.men.final, "Men")

# merge 
tbl_final <- full_join(tbl_women, tbl_men, by = "term") %>%
  rename(`Risk factor` = term)

tbl_final
## # A tibble: 7 × 5
##   `Risk factor`                      Women_HR_CI     Women_Pct Men_HR_CI Men_Pct
##   <chr>                              <chr>           <chr>     <chr>     <chr>  
## 1 Age (per 1 year)                   1.08 (1.03, 1.… +7.5%     1.07 (1.… +6.6%  
## 2 Systolic BP (per 10 mmHg)          1.28 (1.16, 1.… +28.0%    1.41 (1.… +41.4% 
## 3 BP meds (Yes vs No)                1.82 (0.03, 12… +81.7%    2824.27 … +28232…
## 4 Diabetes (Yes vs No)               1.81 (0.65, 5.… +81.4%    5.03 (2.… +403.4%
## 5 Current smoker (Yes vs No)         1.45 (0.83, 2.… +45.2%    1.97 (1.… +96.5% 
## 6 Cardiovascular Disease (Yes vs No) 1.65 (0.69, 3.… +65.3%    1.02 (0.… +2.4%  
## 7 SBP × BP meds interaction          0.99 (0.79, 1.… -1.0%     0.65 (0.… -35.0%
gt_tbl <- tbl_final %>%
  mutate(
    Men_HR_CI = if_else(
      `Risk factor` == "BP meds (Yes vs No)", "\u2020", Men_HR_CI),
    Men_Pct = if_else(`Risk factor`=="BP meds (Yes vs No)","\u2020",Men_Pct)) %>% 
  gt() %>%
  tab_header(
    title = "Hazard Ratios and Percent Change in Stroke Hazard by Sex") %>%
  cols_label(
    `Risk factor` = "Risk factor",
    Men_HR_CI = "HR (95% CI;P-val)",
    Men_Pct = "%Δ in Hazard",
    Women_HR_CI = "HR (95% CI;P-val)",
    Women_Pct = "%Δ in Hazard") %>%
  tab_spanner(label = "Men", columns = c(Men_HR_CI, Men_Pct)) %>%
  tab_spanner(label = "Women", columns = c(Women_HR_CI, Women_Pct)) %>%
  cols_align(align = "left", columns = `Risk factor`) %>%
  cols_align(align = "center",
    columns = c(Men_HR_CI, Men_Pct, Women_HR_CI, Women_Pct)) %>%
  tab_style(
    style = cell_text(weight = "bold"),
    locations = cells_column_labels(everything())) %>%
  tab_style(
    style = cell_text(weight = "bold"),
    locations = cells_column_spanners()) %>% 
  # by risk factor name
  # tab_style(
  #   style = cell_text(weight = "bold"),
  #   locations = cells_body(columns = `Risk factor`)) %>% 
  tab_footnote(
    footnote = md("\u2020 Because the BP \u00d7 BP medication 
                  interaction was significant in men, the effect of BP meds 
                  depends on BP and is not summarized as a single hazard ratio."),
    locations = cells_body(columns = c(Men_HR_CI, Men_Pct),
      rows = `Risk factor` %in% c("BP meds (Yes vs No)", 
                                  "SBP × BP meds interaction"))) %>% 
  tab_options(
    table.width = pct(88),
    table.font.size = px(13),
    heading.align = "left",
    data_row.padding = px(5),
    table.border.top.width = px(2),
    table.border.bottom.width = px(2)) 

gt_tbl
Hazard Ratios and Percent Change in Stroke Hazard by Sex
Risk factor
Women
Men
HR (95% CI;P-val) %Δ in Hazard HR (95% CI;P-val) %Δ in Hazard
Age (per 1 year) 1.08 (1.03, 1.12; p=<0.01) +7.5% 1.07 (1.03, 1.11; p=<0.01) +6.6%
Systolic BP (per 10 mmHg) 1.28 (1.16, 1.41; p=<0.01) +28.0% 1.41 (1.26, 1.59; p=<0.01) +41.4%
BP meds (Yes vs No) 1.82 (0.03, 126.56; p=0.78) +81.7% †1 †1
Diabetes (Yes vs No) 1.81 (0.65, 5.05; p=0.25) +81.4% 5.03 (2.18, 11.61; p=<0.01) +403.4%
Current smoker (Yes vs No) 1.45 (0.83, 2.55; p=0.19) +45.2% 1.97 (1.05, 3.67; p=0.034) +96.5%
Cardiovascular Disease (Yes vs No) 1.65 (0.69, 3.94; p=0.26) +65.3% 1.02 (0.36, 2.95; p=0.964) +2.4%
SBP × BP meds interaction 0.99 (0.79, 1.25; p=0.93) -1.0% 0.65 (0.44, 0.97; p=0.036)1 -35.0%1
1 † Because the BP × BP medication interaction was significant in men, the effect of BP meds depends on BP and is not summarized as a single hazard ratio.

10yr probability of stroke based on different risk profiles

risk_profiles <- tibble(
  scenario = c(
    "Baseline",
    "High BP",
    "Diabetes",
    "Diabetes + High BP",
    "Smoker + Diabetes + High BP"),
  AGE = c(40, 40, 40, 40, 40),   # placeholder, replaced below
  SYSBP_10 = c(12, 16, 12, 16, 16),   # 120 and 160 mmHg
  DIABETES = factor(c("No", "No", "Yes", "Yes", "Yes"),
                    levels = c("No", "Yes")),
  CURSMOKE = factor(c("No", "No", "No", "No", "Yes"),
                    levels = c("No", "Yes")),
  PREVCHD = factor(c("No", "No", "No", "No", "No"),
                   levels = c("No", "Yes")),
  BPMEDS = factor(c("No", "No", "No", "No", "No"),
                  levels = c("No", "Yes")))

pred_data <- bind_rows(
  risk_profiles %>% mutate(AGE = 40),
  risk_profiles %>% mutate(AGE = 50),
  risk_profiles %>% mutate(AGE = 60)) %>%
  mutate(age_group = AGE)
make_preds <- function(model, sex_label, ages = c(40, 50, 60)) {
  out <- lapply(ages, function(a) {
    nd <- risk_profiles %>%
      mutate(AGE = a)

    sf <- survfit(model, newdata = nd)
    surv_10y <- as.numeric(summary(sf, times = 10*365.25, extend = TRUE)$surv)

    nd %>%
      transmute(
        sex = sex_label,
        scenario,
        age = paste("Age", a),
        risk_10y = round(100 * (1 - surv_10y), 2)) #100%
    })
  bind_rows(out)
  }

women_results <- make_preds(cox.women.final, "Women")
men_results   <- make_preds(cox.men.final, "Men")

risk_table <- bind_rows(women_results, men_results) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = age, values_from = risk_10y)

risk_table
## # A tibble: 10 × 5
##    sex   scenario                    `Age 40` `Age 50` `Age 60`
##    <chr> <chr>                          <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl>
##  1 Women Baseline                        0.4      0.82     1.68
##  2 Women High BP                         1.06     2.17     4.43
##  3 Women Diabetes                        0.72     1.47     3.02
##  4 Women Diabetes + High BP              1.91     3.91     7.89
##  5 Women Smoker + Diabetes + High BP     2.77     5.62    11.2 
##  6 Men   Baseline                        0.35     0.66     1.24
##  7 Men   High BP                         1.38     2.59     4.85
##  8 Men   Diabetes                        1.73     3.26     6.07
##  9 Men   Diabetes + High BP              6.75    12.4     22.2 
## 10 Men   Smoker + Diabetes + High BP    12.8     22.9     38.9

Check that function is behaving correctly

women_50_base <- tibble(
  AGE = 50,
  SYSBP_10 = 12,   # 120 mmHg
  DIABETES = factor("No", levels = c("No", "Yes")),
  CURSMOKE = factor("No", levels = c("No", "Yes")),
  PREVCHD = factor("No", levels = c("No", "Yes")),
  BPMEDS = factor("No", levels = c("No", "Yes")))

sf_check <- survfit(cox.women.final, newdata = women_50_base)
1-summary(sf_check, times = 10*365.25, extend = TRUE)$surv
## [1] 0.008157003

Risk Profiles Table

risk_gt_df <- risk_table %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("Age"), names_to = "age",
               values_to = "risk") %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = sex, values_from = risk) %>%
  rename(Scenario = scenario) %>%
  mutate(
    Scenario = factor(
      Scenario,
      levels = c(
        "Baseline",
        "High BP",
        "Diabetes",
        "Diabetes + High BP",
        "Smoker + Diabetes + High BP")),
    age = factor(age, levels = c("Age 40", "Age 50", "Age 60"))) %>%
  arrange(Scenario, age) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = age,
    values_from = c(Women, Men),
    names_vary = "slowest")

risk_gt_df
## # A tibble: 5 × 7
##   Scenario               `Women_Age 40` `Men_Age 40` `Women_Age 50` `Men_Age 50`
##   <fct>                           <dbl>        <dbl>          <dbl>        <dbl>
## 1 Baseline                         0.4          0.35           0.82         0.66
## 2 High BP                          1.06         1.38           2.17         2.59
## 3 Diabetes                         0.72         1.73           1.47         3.26
## 4 Diabetes + High BP               1.91         6.75           3.91        12.4 
## 5 Smoker + Diabetes + H…           2.77        12.8            5.62        22.9 
## # ℹ 2 more variables: `Women_Age 60` <dbl>, `Men_Age 60` <dbl>
risk_gt <- risk_gt_df %>%
  gt() %>%
  tab_header(
    title = "Predicted 10-Year Probability of Stroke by Risk Profile and Sex") %>%
  cols_label(
    Scenario = "Risk profile",
    `Women_Age 40` = "Women",
    `Men_Age 40` = "Men",
    `Women_Age 50` = "Women",
    `Men_Age 50` = "Men",
    `Women_Age 60` = "Women",
    `Men_Age 60` = "Men") %>%
  tab_spanner(label = "Age 40",columns = c(`Women_Age 40`, `Men_Age 40`)) %>%
  tab_spanner(label = "Age 50",columns = c(`Women_Age 50`, `Men_Age 50`)) %>%
  tab_spanner(label = "Age 60",columns = c(`Women_Age 60`, `Men_Age 60`)) %>%
  fmt_number(columns = -Scenario, decimals = 1) %>%
  cols_align(align = "left", columns = Scenario) %>%
  cols_align( align = "center", columns = -Scenario) %>%
  tab_style(
    style = cell_text(weight = "bold"),
    locations = cells_column_labels(everything())) %>%
  tab_style(
    style = cell_text(weight = "bold"),
    locations = cells_column_spanners()) %>% 
  tab_options(
    heading.align = "left",
    table.font.size = px(14),
    data_row.padding = px(5),
    table.border.top.width = px(2),
    table.border.bottom.width = px(2))

risk_gt
Predicted 10-Year Probability of Stroke by Risk Profile and Sex
Risk profile
Age 40
Age 50
Age 60
Women Men Women Men Women Men
Baseline 0.4 0.3 0.8 0.7 1.7 1.2
High BP 1.1 1.4 2.2 2.6 4.4 4.8
Diabetes 0.7 1.7 1.5 3.3 3.0 6.1
Diabetes + High BP 1.9 6.8 3.9 12.4 7.9 22.1
Smoker + Diabetes + High BP 2.8 12.8 5.6 22.9 11.2 38.9

Plot the 10yr probs

plot_df <- risk_table %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("Age"),
    names_to = "age",
    values_to = "risk") %>%
  mutate(
    scenario = factor(
      scenario,
      levels = c("Baseline", "High BP", "Diabetes", 
                 "Diabetes + High BP", "Smoker")),
    age = factor(age, levels = c("Age 40", "Age 50", "Age 60")))

ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = scenario, y = risk, fill = age)) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), width = 0.7) +
  facet_wrap(~ sex) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "Age 40" = "#0072B2",
    "Age 50" = "#E69F00",
    "Age 60" = "#009E73")) +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "Predicted 10-year stroke risk (%)",
    fill = "Age") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "top",
    strip.background = element_rect(fill = "white"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 20, hjust = 1))

Secondary Analysis

“how much change there is in the risk factors over in the 3 periods of data collection”

# # change across periods
# rf_change <- fmhst_filt %>%
#   group_by(SEX) %>%
#   summarise(
#     # SYSBP
#     SYSBP_p1_med = median(SYSBP_PERIOD1, na.rm = TRUE),
#     SYSBP_p2_med = median(SYSBP_PERIOD2, na.rm = TRUE),
#     SYSBP_p3_med = median(SYSBP_PERIOD3, na.rm = TRUE),
#     SYSBP_change_abs = SYSBP_p3_med - SYSBP_p1_med,
#     SYSBP_change_pct = (SYSBP_change_abs / SYSBP_p1_med) * 100,
#     
#     # BMI
#   )
